# Olivers Consulting

Olivers Consulting helps UK ecommerce and wholesale businesses automate repetitive data and admin work: supplier files, product listings, stock updates, reporting, invoices, catalogue content and document processing.

Founder: Henry Oliver. Henry has 11 years running multi-channel ecommerce, formerly PwC and Citi, and built automations first inside his own 200,000 SKU operation across Shopify, Amazon and eBay.

Core offer:
- Free 30-minute process review
- Supplier data pipelines
- Reports that write themselves
- Channel and stock sync
- Order-to-invoice admin
- Catalogue content and document engine
- Fixed-price builds
- Optional care plan

Key proof point:
- Supplier-file process reduced from 8 hours to 2 minutes with human review and bad rows flagged before publishing.

Important URLs:
- Homepage: https://oliversconsulting.co.uk/
- Articles: https://oliversconsulting.co.uk/articles/
- Sitemap: https://oliversconsulting.co.uk/sitemap.xml

Published articles:
- Why stock sync fails between Shopify, Amazon and eBay: https://oliversconsulting.co.uk/articles/why-stock-sync-fails-shopify-amazon-ebay/
- The Monday report your ecommerce team should not be building by hand: https://oliversconsulting.co.uk/articles/monday-report-automation/
- Before you hire another ecommerce admin, check the process first: https://oliversconsulting.co.uk/articles/before-hiring-ecommerce-admin/
- How to automate supplier price lists for Shopify, Amazon and wholesale channels: https://oliversconsulting.co.uk/articles/automate-supplier-price-lists/

Topic clusters:
- Supplier data automation: price lists, catalogues, bad rows, margin rules and publishing checks
- Reporting automation: Monday packs, stock cover, channel performance and exception reports
- Channel operations: Shopify, Amazon, eBay, stock sync, bundles, buffers and reconciliation
- Wholesale and finance admin: Sage, Xero, QuickBooks, PDFs, portals and order-to-invoice work
- Hiring versus automation: process checks before adding admin headcount

Editorial focus:
- Practical automation advice for UK ecommerce and wholesale operators
- Commercial SEO articles for problem-aware buyers
- Founder-led operator notes based on real multi-channel ecommerce experience
- No generic automation hype, invented case studies or keyword stuffing
